lesson: Call asyncio.run(coro()) once at the top level to drive an event loop; await inside async def suspends until the awaited coroutine/future resolves, returning its value directly.
asyncio.gather(*coros) runs coroutines concurrently and returns results in the SAME order as the input args, regardless of which one finishes first.
task: gather preserves input order under concurrency

lesson: Wrap a slow coroutine in asyncio.wait_for(coro, timeout=t) and catch asyncio.TimeoutError; wait_for cancels the inner coroutine/task automatically on timeout.
Build an async context manager with @contextlib.asynccontextmanager and a try/finally around yield so cleanup runs in `async with` even when the body raises.

task.cancel() only raises asyncio.CancelledError inside the task at its next await point; re-raise it after cleanup in a try/except so task.cancelled() reports True instead of swallowing the cancellation.
Bound fan-out concurrency by acquiring a shared asyncio.Semaphore(n) inside each worker's `async with sem:` block; gather still preserves result order while capping simultaneous work at n.
Wrap a task in asyncio.shield() before wait_for so an outer timeout only abandons the wait, not the task; await it again for the real result.
task: protect a critical task from outer timeout with asyncio.shield

lesson: Implement __aiter__/__anext__ raising StopAsyncIteration for manual async-iterator state; pass return_exceptions=True to gather so one failing coroutine's exception becomes a value instead of cancelling running siblings.
task: custom async iterator plus non-cancelling gather with return_exceptions
